======= RMA04028William McGuire papers16-4-4028Compiled by RMC StaffDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections

This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor) >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
William McGuire papers
McGuire, William, 1920-
16-4-4028
5.5 cubic feet. 5.5 cubic feet.
1946-2004. Printed material, reports, correspondence, photographs. Collection includes consulting files for projects including Armco Steel Corporation design of rigid frame buildings and roof systems; Power Reactor Development Corp. study of containment of fast breeder reactor; Buckminster Fuller design of Museum of Modern Art exhibit; Gibbs & Hill prestressed concrete nuclear containment vessel studies;Central Hudson Gas & Electric Corp. implosion of large steam generator; National Bureau of Standards structural integrity of U.S. Olympic structure, Lake Placid and the investigation of the Hyatt Regency Walkway collapse; C. Lindbergh & Associates review of the design of the structure of a very large engine test facility in Tullahoma, TN; Visual Edge Ltd development of ELRFD, an electronic version of the AISC LRFD specification; OSHA the L'Ambiance Plaza collapse; and work on the Green Bank Telescope in West Virginia. Also Cornell projects, including the Fall Creek Suspension Bridge; student notes; and lecture notes for Cornell engineering classes. Also plans for the Hyatt Regency Hotel (Kansas City, Missouri); for L'Ambiance Plaza (Bridgeport, Connecticut), and for the Triphammer Falls Foot Bridge. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD ? (unprocessed) ======= ? (unprocessed) >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 7994192
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Janette Park
Date completed:
April 2018
EAD encoding:
RMC Staff, April 2018
Date modified:
Fredrika Loew, April 2018
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering. Bill McGuire, an expert in steel structures, received a B.S. degree from Bucknell University in 1942 and, after serving in the U.S. Navy during World War II, an M.C.E. degree from Cornell in 1947. From 1947 to 1949, he worked for Jackson & Moreland Engineers as a structural designer of power plants and atomic energy projects. He returned to Cornell in 1949, was promoted to associate professor in 1952 and professor in 1960. He served as director of Civil Engineering from 1966-1968, and retired in 1989. He was the author of two influential textbooks: "Steel Structures" and "Matrix Structural Analysis." His consulting as a licensed professional engineer included the design of special structures and the investigation of structural failures. He was deeply involved in the planning design, upgrade, and maintenance of the radio telescope at Arecibo. He was also the co-designer of the Fall Creek Suspension Bridge.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Collection includes consulting files for projects including Armco Steel Corporation design of rigid frame buildings and roof systems; Power Reactor Development Corp. study of containment of fast breeder reactor; Buckminster Fuller design of Museum of Modern Art exhibit; Gibbs & Hill prestressed concrete nuclear containment vessel studies;Central Hudson Gas & Electric Corp. implosion of large steam generator; National Bureau of Standards structural integrity of U.S. Olympic structure, Lake Placid and the investigation of the Hyatt Regency Walkway collapse; C. Lindbergh & Associates review of the design of the structure of a very large engine test facility in Tullahoma, TN; Visual Edge Ltd development of ELRFD, an electronic version of the AISC LRFD specification; OSHA the L'Ambiance Plaza collapse; and work on the Green Bank Telescope in West Virginia. Also Cornell projects, including the Fall Creek Suspension Bridge; student notes; and lecture notes for Cornell engineering classes.
Also plans for the Hyatt Regency Hotel (Kansas City, Missouri); for L'Ambiance Plaza (Bridgeport, Connecticut), and for the Triphammer Falls Foot Bridge.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Consulting Files
Series II. Green Bank Telescope (GBT)
Series III. Cornell material
Series IV. William McGuire's Drawings
Cite As:
William McGuire Papers, #16-4-4028. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Astronomy -- West Virginia -- Green Bank. Bridges -- New York (State) -- Ithaca. Building failures -- Connecticut -- Bridgeport -- Investigation. Building failures -- Missouri -- Kansas City -- Investigation. Structural failures -- Investigation. Nuclear reactors. Power reactors. Structural engineering -- Study and teaching. Civil engineering -- Study and teaching. Fuller, R. Buckminster (Richard Buckminster), 1895-1983. Armco Steel Corporation Power Reactor Development Corporation Museum of Modern Art (New York, N.Y.) -- : Exhibitions. Cornell University. College of Civil and Environmental Engineering Gibbs & Hill Central Hudson Gas & Electric Corporation United States. National Bureau of Standards Kansas City Hyatt Regency United States. Occupational Safety and Health Administration L\'Ambiance Plaza (Bridgeport, Conn.) C. Lindbergh & Associates National Radio Astronomy Observatory (U.S.) Olympic Games (22nd ) (Location of meeting: Lake Placid, N.Y.). Date of meeting or treaty signing: 1980 :.)
